expectations.text: Output contains all 6 rounds (Round 1 through Round 6)
expectations.evidence: The output uses a standard architecture decision format with sections like Rationale, Architecture, Risks. No round-based structure (Round 1 through Round 6) is present.

expectations.text: Architect, Operator, and CTO personas all appear and are distinct
expectations.evidence: No distinct personas appear in the output. The document is written from a single advisory voice.

expectations.text: Every challenge in Round 2 has a severity rating (critical/significant/minor)
expectations.evidence: There is no Round 2. The Risks and Mitigations table uses Likelihood (Low/Medium/High) but not severity ratings of critical/significant/minor.

expectations.text: Round 2 challenges are categorized (reliability, complexity, migration-risk, team-capability, cost, dependency, security)
expectations.evidence: There is no Round 2 and no challenges categorized using the specified taxonomy.

expectations.text: Round 4 references specific mitigations from Round 3, not just repeating Round 2 challenges
expectations.evidence: No Round 3 or Round 4 structure exists in the output.

expectations.text: Round 6 contains one of the 5 valid verdicts: proceed, proceed-with-conditions, prototype-first, defer, reject
expectations.evidence: No Round 6 exists and none of the five valid verdict keywords appear anywhere in the document.

expectations.text: Metrics table present with all 8 metrics filled in (challengesRaised, stressTestsRaised, challengesSurvived, challengesConceded, mitigationsHeld, mitigationsBroken, proposalModified, confidenceScore)
expectations.evidence: No debate metrics table is present. The document contains other tables (extraction signals, conflict resolution, risks) but none with the 8 required metrics.

expectations.text: Concrete action items with who/what/when structure
expectations.evidence: The document has a week-by-week architecture plan (Weeks 1-2, 3-5, 6-7, 8) with tasks, but no assigned owners ('who'). The structure is what/when but missing who.

expectations.text: Round 5 either modifies the original proposal or explicitly justifies keeping it unchanged
expectations.evidence: No Round 5 structure exists. The document does justify the ActionCable recommendation over alternatives, but not within a Round 5 framework.

expectations.text: Round 5 includes fallback/escape-hatch plans for surviving challenges
expectations.evidence: No Round 5 exists. The document does mention AnyCable as an 'escape hatch' and includes extraction thresholds, but these are not within the expected round-based structure.
